This rare and unique species is considered to be a hybrid between Tsuga and Keteleeria. As such, it is placed in a distinct subcategory of the Pinaceae family. The classification of × Tsugoketeleeria longibracteata is primarily based on its botanical characteristics and morphology. Further research is needed to confirm its exact classification within the Pinaceae family.

intro: × Tsugoketeleeria longibracteata, also known as Tsuga-Tsugae hybrid, is a sterile intergeneric hybrid plant that belongs to the family Pinaceae, displaying characteristics of both Tsuga (hemlock spruce) and Keteleeria (Chinese hemlock spruce). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of × Tsugoketeleeria longibracteata include various terpenoids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, such as Taxifolin, Quercetin, and Ferulic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, this plant is typically prepared as a decoction, tea, or powder, and is often used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, as well as to boost the immune system.
